title: Sync release metadata and recheck public distribution

sources.reason: Declares the release tag and supply-chain digests consumed by the public distribution check.
Defines the anonymous public tag, release metadata, and installer distribution verification.
Exposes make check-release-distribution and the repository quality gate.
Documents the release and public distribution workflow exercised by the check.
Defines the Work Item lifecycle, generated status handling, and required finish evidence.
Owns the nested-check environment sanitizer used by AI finish and other governance subprocesses.
Covers the finish lifecycle and the environment contract that prevents generated bytecode from polluting ownership checks.

riskAssessment.reason: The local checkout must be evaluated against the current anonymous public release contract without changing release metadata.

executionDecision.reason: The user confirmed continuation after the initial Preflight Pause; this round is a bounded verification and evidence update.

preReviewWarnings: Reviewers should distinguish public-access failure from stale local release metadata and confirm that no out-of-scope release file was changed.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make check-release-distribution passes from the latest origin/main-based branch and reports the public release contract as internally consistent.
The evidence records that the repository is anonymously reachable and that release.json agrees with the highest public tag and published supply-chain digests.
The Work Item is archived only after all required governance and project checks pass; nested AI checks do not create ownership-visible bytecode in source directories.

intent.problem: The previous checkout failed public release verification while the repository was private, and after visibility was restored the check exposed that the local checkout still pointed to v0.5.24 while the public highest tag was v0.5.25.
constraints: Start from origin/main so the verification reflects the current published mainline.
Do not silently merge, rewrite, or overwrite the user's stale local main branch.
Keep the implementation limited to the release verification evidence and the nested-check bytecode side-effect fix; defer coverage improvements and schema warning cleanup to later rounds.
intent.rationale: A release score should be based on the latest mainline and a genuinely anonymous public distribution check, not on a stale checkout or ambient local credentials.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: anonymous public tag and release metadata access
scenarioCoverage.reason: Anonymous public tag and release metadata access passed.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: release.json releaseTag matches the highest public tag
scenarioCoverage.reason: The current origin/main-based branch reports v0.5.25, matching the highest public tag.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: published SBOM and provenance digests match release metadata
scenarioCoverage.reason: SBOM and provenance checks passed against the published release metadata.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: project-lint does not create source-tree bytecode that breaks diff ownership
scenarioCoverage.reason: Nested AI checks suppress bytecode generation even when ambient Make variables are present.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed continuation and approved the bounded Makefile governance fix.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
